summary: Matched the common fighter HammerTurn state as a new C source file, removing the corresponding assembly from ft_0C31.s. The PR added ftCo_HammerTurn.c/.h, wired the object into obj_files.mk, moved HammerTurn prototypes out of the generic ft_0C31.h header, and refined ftCommonData::x6B0 from UNK_T to int to support the matched timing/math logic. The match report shows the new file at 740 bytes and 100.00% across ftCo_800C576C, ftCo_800C57B4, ftCo_HammerTurn_Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerTurn.c with matched implementations for ftCo_800C576C, static ftCo_800C57B4, ftCo_HammerTurn_Anim, ftCo_HammerTurn_IASA, ftCo_HammerTurn_Phys, and ftCo_HammerTurn_Coll.
Removed the corresponding HammerTurn assembly and associated local constants/assert strings from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerTurn.h with public prototypes for the HammerTurn entry point and state callbacks.
Removed HammerTurn declarations from src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h, leaving later ft_0C31 symbols there and moving HammerTurn ownership to its dedicated header.
Added ftCo_HammerTurn.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31.s object.
Included ftCo_HammerTurn.h from ftCo_Init.c and ftCo_HammerWait.c so users reference the dedicated HammerTurn header.
Changed ftCommonData field x6B0 from UNK_T to int, matching its use as a frame/count divisor in HammerTurn logic.
Adjusted ftCo_Init.c include style by including ftCo_WarpStar.h from the same ftCommon include group rather than via ftCommon/ftCo_WarpStar.h.
smart_moves: Split the newly matched state into a dedicated ftCo_HammerTurn.c/.h instead of leaving prototypes in the broad ft_0C31.h placeholder header.
Kept ftCo_800C57B4 static in the C file, reflecting that it is an internal helper for HammerTurn setup rather than a public API.
Used existing adjacent common-state helpers rather than duplicating behavior: HammerTurn_Phys delegates to ft_80084F3C and HammerTurn_Coll delegates to ftCo_HammerWait_Coll.
Preserved build ordering by inserting ftCo_HammerTurn.c.o immediately before the remaining ft_0C31.s.o, allowing partial asm-to-C migration of the file.
Converted p_ftCommonData->x6B0 to int based on concrete arithmetic use in the matched code, improving struct knowledge beyond placeholder typing.
Modeled the animation turn using named fields and helpers: fp->mv.co.walk.fast_anim_frame, fp->x2222_b6, fp->parts[part].x4_jobj2, FtPart_TransN, HSD_JObjSetRotationY, and facing_dir negation.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous action-state block is matched, remove only that block from the asm file and leave subsequent unmatched symbols in place; obj_files.mk can compile the new C object alongside the reduced asm object.
Action-state headers should live near their ftCommon state source files once matched, while generic address-range headers like ft_0C31.h should shrink as symbols are decompiled.
Type fields in shared structs from actual matched behavior: x6B0 is not just an opaque pointer-like UNK_T here, but an int used in frame-count conversion and division.
Simple state callbacks often match as wrappers around existing engine/common helpers, so check neighboring state files such as HammerWait and HammerWalk for reusable patterns.
For animation code involving JObj rotation, the C source can rely on HSD_JObjSetRotationY rather than reproducing low-level assert and dirty-matrix sequences seen in assembly.
The fast_anim_frame countdown is central to HammerTurn: decrement it, optionally rotate the TransN JObj over p_ftCommonData->x6B0 frames, then flip facing_dir and exit when the timer reaches zero.
naming_conventions: Dedicated common fighter state files use ftCo_StateName.c and ftCo_StateName.h, here ftCo_HammerTurn.c and ftCo_HammerTurn.h.
Public state callbacks follow ftCo_HammerTurn_Anim, ftCo_HammerTurn_IASA, ftCo_HammerTurn_Phys, and ftCo_HammerTurn_Coll.
Unidentified address-based helpers retain ftCo_800Cxxxx names, but file-local helpers can be marked static when no longer needed outside the matched source.
The motion-state enum name used in the state change call is ftCo_MS_HammerTurn.
Known fighter fields use descriptive nested names where available, such as mv.co.walk.fast_anim_frame and facing_dir.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched 740 bytes total with reported 100.00% scores for the file and all six functions.
Replaced integer-to-float conversion sequences around p_ftCommonData->x6B0 with normal C arithmetic using an int field and float expression.
Used M_PI in the rotation interpolation expression corresponding to the assembly constant near 2.1426990032196045 plus its double tail data.
The source comment in ftCo_800C57B4 notes a matching constraint: the local variable flags cannot be moved below the Fighter* fp declaration, suggesting an inline/order-sensitive compiler artifact.
Used RETURN_IF(ftCo_800C5A50(gobj)) for the IASA wrapper, matching the short call-and-return assembly shape.
Used HSD_JObjSetRotationY(jobj, rot_y), which encapsulates the underlying JObj rotation write, quaternion flag assertion behavior, and matrix dirtying seen in the removed assembly.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800C576C or ftCo_800C57B4 to semantic HammerTurn helper names.
Search for other uses of p_ftCommonData->x6B0 to confirm whether it consistently represents a HammerTurn/hammer animation frame count.
Inspect adjacent HammerWait and HammerWalk PRs for shared hammer-state patterns and potential further header cleanup.
Check whether later decomp work replaces x2222_b6 with a named bitfield for model or rotation behavior.
